posts.alt_text:
For their Tuscan wedding ceremony, @KatieCBoulter worked with British bridal designer @CarolineCastigliano on the dress she would marry fellow tennis player @AlexDeMinaur in. The couple reflects that celebrating family was at the heart of all their festivities, and that included Katie's bridal fashion. A piece of fabric from Katie's late grandmother's wedding dress had been hand-woven into her custom gown. “I wanted to feel like she was with me in Italy,” the bride explains. “It felt like a fairy tale," Alex recalls about his first glimpse of Katie walking down the aisle. 

Alex wore a @RalphLauren, double-breasted tuxedo. “I wanted to feel like James Bond,” he says. Following the ceremony held against the estate ruins, the couple invited their guests into a long-running debate of theirs during dinner: Whose favorite pasta reigns supreme? Katie's penne alla vodka and Alex’s cacio e pepe were put to a guest vote, and the bride's dish emerged victorious. The celebration carried on late into the night, with the live band and DJ keeping the dance floor full until 4:30a.m. “It felt like our own movie," reflects Alex. Tap the link for more photos and details on their romantic Italian wedding. 

Photos: 
@KatieJulia_

posts.caption:
Having the courage to step into the unknown—that is the commanding trait of our 'Audacity' issue. It is something our cover star @chua.tanya has never shied away from, even at the most tumultuous point of her career.
With little to no support for her musical direction back then, the esteemed Singaporean singer-songwriter was determined to escape to Taiwan with only three months worth of rent in her bank account. "I had just won a Golden Melody Award, but it’s not like that helps pay the bills. I let myself disintegrate entirely to find myself again. It was a turning point for me because it allowed me to ponder this question of why we are so afraid of failure," she reminisces.
For #VogueSingapore's July/Aug 'Audacity' issue, Chua lays herself bare, sharing with us the mettle behind her musical vision, her boundless curiosity for life and trying out new things. Read the full story now in the link in bio and preorder a copy, out on newsstands on 22 July.
Editor-in-chief: @monkiepoo

posts.alt_text:
Curiousity never killed the cat. So at 51, @chua.tanya is still doing hardcore gymnastics; an indisputable reflection of what has always been a lifelong resolve—to always try the things that scare you. 

"I’m doing backflips and handstands at an age where most people would be terrified to try. But if you’re constantly fearful of failure, nothing is going to get done. You’ll never do anything interesting or out of the ordinary," our 'Audacity' cover star proclaims. 

In the link in bio, the Singaporean singer-songwriter's narrative may read mercurial yet there was always a steadfast nerve behind it all: be it back when she debuted with her English album in 1997, when she wanted to do a tour dedicated to her less recognised tracks or even when she decided to take up gymnastics later in life. 

The July/Aug 'Audacity' issue is available for pre-order, and will hit newsstands on 22 July.

posts.alt_text:
Having the courage to step into the unknown—that is the commanding trait of our 'Audacity' issue. It is something our cover star @chua.tanya has never shied away from, even at the most tumultuous point of her career.

With little to no support for her musical direction back then, the esteemed Singaporean singer-songwriter was determined to escape to Taiwan with only three months worth of rent in her bank account. "I had just won a Golden Melody Award, but it’s not like that helps pay the bills. I let myself disintegrate entirely to find myself again. It was a turning point for me because it allowed me to ponder this question of why we are so afraid of failure," she reminisces. 

For #VogueSingapore's July/Aug 'Audacity' issue, Chua lays herself bare, sharing with us the mettle behind her musical vision, her boundless curiosity for life and trying out new things. Read the full story now in the link in bio and preorder a copy, out on newsstands on 22 July.

posts.caption:
The streets of Seoul; a cacophony of sounds; a smorgasbord of sights. Earlier this year, the South Korean capital played out as the vibrant locale in which @chanelofficial hosted its replica show for its Métiers d'art 2026—one which @shiiiannn sat front row at. The emerging starlet and #VogueSingapore's digital cover star for July/August has been on the up and up, with roles spanning 'Amoeba', 'We Learn to Breathe in Distant Places' and 'Secondary: The Musical'.
With performances marked by a rare duality—earnest yet incisive, tender yet unflinching—she brings a striking depth to every character she inhabits. Similarly, for her time in Seoul, she brings her introspective nature to various locations that feel intrinsically part of the city's cultural fabric, as #VogueSingapore lenses her in the chaos of a pocha, or in the company of Buchaechum dancers.
In the link in bio and our July/Aug 'Audacity' issue, the Singaporean darling shares more about her craft, dream projects and ties to the fashion world.

posts.caption:
Véronique Manchharam founded @BohemeByVero as an amalgam of philanthropy, sustainability, and exquisite design. From ethically sourced gems, recycled metals, to fundraising initiatives, it never detracts from the jeweller’s free-spirited design language, but acts as a welcome extension.
For her exclusive collaboration with #VogueSingapore, Manchharam crafted the limited edition brooch from 18-carat gold-plated recycled brass; individually sculpting each letter. Adorned with a delicate display of natural pearls and cubic zirconia, the brooch lends itself to the creativity of self-styling: on knitwear or denim to elevate a look, transforming a cap into a statement, and even as a hair moment.
From now until 31 August, the limited edition collaboration will be a welcome gift for the first twenty members who join Club Vogue, or is available for purchase directly from the Bohème by Véro website. Learn more in the link in bio. #VogueSingapore

Step into a summer state of mind with the #VogueSingapore Summer Bag. Summer isn’t simply a matter of reaching for SPF, but taking the moment to reset, unwind, and decompress—and that mentality extends beyond weather or place.
Whether it is taking an ice-cold dip courtesy of @aqua.tula; taking a breath at the @capybarabathing.sg bathhouses; or giving your eyes a moment of rejuvenation thanks to @pixibeauty—our Summer Bag is curating the ultimate holistic reset with local and international brands alike. Immerse yourself in our Summer Bag now, and explore the products and experiences for yourself at the link in bio. #VogueSingapore

posts.caption:
Breaking boundaries in the creative field is not merely about maximalist aesthetics—but forging your own path, whether that may be committing to becoming a creative professional or starting your own business. This particularly rings true for a city where it’s considered an unconventional career, not to mention the job insecurities or trusting in the lengthy process of creation.
For Vogue Singapore’s July/August ‘Audacity’ issue, beauty writer @ailouest speaks to the make-up artists, hairstylists, and nail artists who are melding, warping, and refining the blueprint—offering a look at what it takes to truly go against the grain, as well as what fuels their rebellious spirit.
#VogueSingapore’s latest issue is now out on newsstands.

posts.alt_text:
KAVITAON KA SILSILA AA RAHA HAI @sohotheatre LONDON. 💌🫀

They say everything iconic has once happened at SOHO (@sohotheatre ). The thought that Rajkumari will now be counted among the stories told on that stage still doesn’t feel real.

It’s strange… I can often find words for emotions people have buried deep within them, but when it comes to this show~my own, I always fall short.

Rajkumari has changed my life in ways I don’t think I’ll ever fully be able to put into poetry.

The last time we were in London, we promised we’d return bigger and better.

We kept that promise.

This is our biggest London show yet, and I’d love for you to be in the room with us, to laugh, cry, heal, and become a part of this chapter.

Tickets are in my bio. See you at Soho. 🔗🤍

@tarashna.management @beacon.live

posts.caption:
Still trying to reply to every DM, every message, every blessing… and smiling through all of them. 🥹❤️
The love you’ll have given me & Rohan has been unreal. Thank you for celebrating us like we’re your own family.
And oh my God… the love for our outfits! 🥹 Every message appreciating the tiniest personalized details, every “I noticed this,” every compliment… it truly feels like you guys see us. You saw the thought, the emotion, the intention behind every little choice we made. That means more to us than you’ll ever know. Thank you. ❤️
Everyone’s asking us the same questions—
“How did this happen?”
“How did you get married there?”
“How did you even dream of this?”
The truth is… we don’t have an answer.
Some dreams aren’t planned. They’re blessed. 🤍🕉️
If there’s one person who made all of this possible, it’s Mahadev. We were just lucky enough to be chosen.
From the bottom of our hearts… thank you for every prayer, every tear, every kind word, and every bit of love you’ve sent our way. We don’t take a single bit of it for granted.
And… ये तो सिर्फ ट्रेलर था… पिक्चर अभी बाकी है मेरे दोस्त. ❤️✨

posts.caption:
Rahul Mishra @rahulmishra_7 presented ‘Devi’ at Haute Couture Week, Paris. Drawing inspiration from ancient South Indian sculptures and the divine feminine, the collection was an absolute showstopper.
The lineup effortlessly transformed featherlight fabrics into breathtaking, structured silhouettes. By blending traditional artistry like intricate hand embroidery, zardozi, and dabka with modern design; Mishra beautifully celebrated India's rich artisanal heritage. The collection's contemporary edge was further elevated through brilliant collaborations with clay artisan Sumant Kumar, legendary milliner Stephen Jones, and Tanishq Natural Diamonds, seamlessly breathing new life into centuries-old craftsmanship.
Jewellery by @tanishqjewellery
Visit www.weddingvows.com for latest updates in wedding trends, region based vendors and bridal inspiration.
#weddingvowsmagazine #cardiB #ishaambani #rahulmishra #taylor

posts.caption:
"Listen. I’m not going to be mad at anyone calling me beautiful,” @lupitanyongo says. In Director Christopher Nolan's much-anticipated, much-shrouded blockbuster adaptation of The Odyssey, Nyong'o plays Helen of Troy, the mythic face that "launched a thousand ships." When asked how it sits with her to be playing one of the most beautiful women of all time, Nyong'o responds enthusiastically, "Really well!" While many women struggle to accept a single compliment, the Oscar-winning actress is fully owning her turn as the legendary Greek figure. After all, regal, striking beauty is inextricable from Nyong'o's public identity: as Nakia, the warrior/spy and love of the late Chadwick Boseman's King T'Challa in Black Panther; as the first Black face of Lancôme; and as a Chanel ambassador. But what fans may not realize is that Nyong'o is pulling double duty in the film by portraying Helen's sister Clytemnestra too.
In our July cover story, @michandrews speaks to Nyong'o about starring in the cultural event of the summer, letting her work speak for itself, and how her ongoing battle with uterine fibroids sparked the Make Fibroids Count research campaign. Read the full interview at the link in our bio.
Photographer / Creative Conception: @knngrm
Stylist: @jan.quammie
Creative Direction: @bonatalia
Hair Stylist: @vernonfrancois
Hair Stylist Assistant: @batiseolhair
Makeup Artist: @nickbarose using @chanel.beauty
Makeup Assistant: @golfxmua
Manicurist: Bagir Hasanov
Set Designer: @lane.vineyard

Entertainment Director: @jbake21
Writer: @michandrews
posts.alt_text: From black tie to formal to cocktail, the best-dressed wedding guests know you don’t need to buy something new for every invitation. Instead, invest in one timeless, versatile dress for each major dress code—you’ll always look elegant, spend less, and avoid the endless trend cycle. At the link in our bio, discover a curated, editor-approved look for every wedding on your calendar, from floor-length gowns to beachy midi dresses.

posts.caption:
"Right before the cake cutting, I changed into a long-sleeve Toteme dress from the Resort 2026 collection. I'd tried it on at a work photo shoot and instantly knew I needed it," Jordan Goldberg says. The fashion editor and consultant married Adam Rossman, a manager at KPMG, over a Western-inspired wedding weekend in Texas. Goldberg was every bit a fashion bride, wearing a look from @bevza for the welcome party and a @daniellefrankel gown for the ceremony. To see all of the stylish details in our latest #WhoWhatWearWeddings feature, head to the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
“It’s all fun, weird, and cool.” @belmontcameli is the latest fresh face to earn the title of BookTok Boyfriend, joining the ranks of @connorstorrieofficial, @hudsonwilliamsofficial, and @chrisbriney_.
Following the massive success of @offcampusonprime—which drew a record-breaking 36 million streams in its first 12 days—Cameli’s career has shifted into high gear, from magazine spreads and first pitches at Chicago White Sox games to his latest beauty campaign.
At the link in our bio, Cameli opens up about life after the show’s splashy debut, what fans can expect from season two, and the simple hair routine behind those signature curls.

As @skims expands its retail operation with new locations in London, Dubai and beyond, Skims CEO @JensGrede told WWD the business is often misunderstood. ⁠
⁠
Behind the celebrity campaigns and viral moments, he says Skims is "a fabric company first and foremost." ⁠
⁠
Read more from the interview at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @evangclark
posts.alt_text:
Mugler is putting some muscle into its first dedicated handbag campaign under creative director Miguel Castro Freitas.⁠
⁠
The black-and-white photos by Chris Lensz feature nude female bodybuilder @tatiana.hrle, whose chiseled physique echoes the architectural lines of the Aurora bag — recalling Robert Mapplethorpe’s early ’80s portraits of Lisa Lyon, as well as founder Manfred Thierry Mugler’s glamazon aesthetic.⁠
⁠
Read more about the campaign at WWD.com — #linkinbio ⁠
⁠
Report: @jdiderich

LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton has promoted Yann Musquin to brand managing director of LVMH Fragrance Brands, leading Parfums Givenchy and Kenzo Parfums, WWD has learned.

He will step into his new role on Aug. 3 and will report to Véronique Courtois, chairman and CEO of Parfums Christian Dior and of LVMH’s beauty division, according to an internal memo released on Thursday.

Musquin’s appointment follows the departure of Romain Spitzer for Bottega Veneta, and reflects the French luxury conglomerate’s tradition of recruiting among its ranks.

Read more at WWD.com – #linkinbio 

Report: @jdiderich 

📸 Courtesy of LVMH

posts.caption:
Los Angeles-based body care brand @saltandstone has tapped singer-songwriter Tucker Pillsbury, professionally known as @rolemodel, for a character-driven campaign timed to his third album “Chuck Timely & The Hourglass,” out on Aug. 7.
In the campaign, Pillsbury plays a fictionalized version of himself, riffing on the flawed, slightly unhinged male protagonists of cult cinema.
Read more details at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @tianweizhang
posts.alt_text:
As @skims expands its retail operation with new locations in London, Dubai and beyond, Skims CEO @JensGrede told WWD the business is often misunderstood. ⁠
⁠
Behind the celebrity campaigns and viral moments, he says Skims is "a fabric company first and foremost." ⁠
⁠
Read more from the interview at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @evangclark

posts.caption:
@jonathan.anderson is bringing his career full circle, with plans to stage his first fashion show in Ireland in December.⁠
⁠
The Northern Irish designer plans to unveil his @dior pre-fall 2027 collection at Trinity College in Dublin on Dec. 4, WWD has learned.⁠
⁠
Anderson’s first job in fashion was at the Brown Thomas department store in Dublin, and he has collaborated with brewer Guinness under his J.W. Anderson label, but this will be the first time he’s presented a collection in the country.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @jdiderich

posts.caption:
Hollywood couldn’t have scripted it any better.⁠
⁠
That’s the thinking of the team at @americaneagle, which had the foresight to sign soccer phenom @lamineyamal as a global ambassador in January. Yamal and his Spanish teammates won the FIFA World Cup on Sunday against last year’s champions, Argentina, and the 19-year-old made history as the youngest player to win both the World Cup and the UEFA European Championship.⁠
⁠
American Eagle inked an unprecedented five-year deal with the athlete that includes him being featured in campaigns and collaborating on limited-edition product.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @jeanpalmieri
